warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose: The fukui reactivity descriptors for formaldehyde were computed using DFT at the B3LYP functional with the def2-TZVP basis set (tier 'standard'), gas phase. The charge and multiplicity used for the system were 0 and 1 (neutral singlet). The calculation was performed on a pre-specified structure from input file at '/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz' without further geometry optimization. PySCF 2.x was employed as the DFT backend, with Mulliken partial charges derived at neutral, cationic, and anionic states used for Fukui coefficients. The charges for the neutral molecule were: [-0.2636, 0.0836, 0.0900, 0.0900]. Electrophilicity (f⁺) sums to 1.0000 and nucleophilicity (f⁻) sums similarly for the gas phase molecule, so checks passed for charge consistency. The most electrophilic atom was identified as C (index 2 in the 1-based ordering) with f⁺ = 0.70839; the most nucleophilic site was assigned to O with f⁻ = 0.46742. Methodological trust integrity checks recognized warnings about larger numbers around null charge migrations flattened surface 'errors' flagged amid solution.
